hints: An INNER JOIN drops any employee who doesn't match a branch. You need a join that keeps every row from employee regardless of whether it matches.
Swap JOIN for LEFT JOIN. Everything else about the query — the ON condition, the columns, the ORDER BY — stays the same.
Full shape: SELECT e.first_name, b.branch_name FROM employee e LEFT JOIN branch b ON e.branch_id = b.branch_id ORDER BY e.employee_id; Ryan's branch_name comes back NULL instead of him disappearing.
